message:
fix(abi): patch glibc's _IO_list_all to prevent exit-time segfault (bd-faew)

When LD_PRELOAD interposes stdio, binaries without COPY relocations for
_IO_list_all crash during exit because glibc's _IO_flush_all_lockp
iterates glibc's internal _IO_list_all containing invalid FILE structures.

The fix uses dlvsym(RTLD_NEXT) to find glibc's _IO_list_all during startup
and sets it to NULL, preventing glibc from iterating corrupt structures.
Binaries with COPY relocations continue using our exported _IO_list_all.

Root cause: glibc's internal code accesses _IO_list_all directly (not
through PLT), so our exported symbol only affects binaries that reference
it explicitly. This patch handles the common case of binaries that don't.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3ni1","title":"printf engine: implement POSIX positional format specifiers (%n$s, %n$d)","description":"## Problem
FrankenLibC's printf engine does not support POSIX positional format specifiers (`%n$s`, `%n$d`, `%n$*m$d`, etc). Programs that use these specifiers get garbled or incorrect output.

## Evidence
`strace -c` under LD_PRELOAD produces lines like:
```
%1$*2$.2f %1$*2$.6f %1$*2$lu %1$*2$lu %1$*2$.0lu %1$s
```
instead of properly formatted syscall statistics. strace internally uses positional specifiers for column alignment.

## Impact
HIGH — any program using positional printf (glibc extension, POSIX.1-2001) gets wrong output. Affected programs include: strace, gawk, some localized programs (gettext translations often use positional args to reorder parameters).

## POSIX Positional Syntax
`%n$` — use the nth argument (1-based) for this conversion.
`%n$*m$` — use the mth argument for field width, nth for value.
Example: `printf("%2$s is %1$d", 42, "answer")` -> `answer is 42`

## Implementation
File: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/stdio/printf.rs (or wherever the format engine lives)
1. Detect `%` followed by digits followed by `$` in the format string
2. Parse the positional index (1-based)
3. Use the corresponding argument from the va_list
4. Support `*n$` for positional width/precision parameters

## Testing
- printf("%2$s is %1$d", 42, "answer") -> "answer is 42"
- printf("%1$d %1$d %1$d", 42) -> "42 42 42" (reuse same arg)
- printf("%1$*2$.2f", 3.14, 10) -> "      3.14"
- strace -c under LD_PRELOAD should produce correct column-aligned output

## Acceptance Criteria
Positional format specifiers work correctly. strace -c output is properly formatted under LD_PRELOAD.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-03T18:47:10.842531931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-04T00:36:39.106662008Z","closed_at":"2026-04-04T00:36:39.106248295Z","close_reason":"Implemented positional printf specifiers; strace -c output formatted correctly under LD_PRELOAD","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["correctness","printf","stdio"],"comments":[{"id":1378,"issue_id":"bd-3ni1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implemented POSIX positional printf specifiers across parser/extractor/render paths. Added explicit value/width/precision indexes in crates/frankenlibc-core/src/stdio/printf.rs, typed positional va_list planning in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdio_abi.rs, and propagated positional extraction support to err/wchar/syslog helpers. Added regression tests in crates/frankenlibc-core/src/stdio/printf.rs and crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/stdio_abi_test.rs for reordered values, reused arguments, and positional width/precision. Verification: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_check_lavendermarsh_positional cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --tests PASS; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_test_lavendermarsh_positional cargo test -p frankenlibc-core test_parse_positional -- --nocapture PASS; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_test_lavendermarsh_positional cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test stdio_abi_test positional -- --nocapture PASS; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_ws_check_lavendermarsh_positional cargo check --workspace --all-targets PASS; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_ws_clippy_lavendermarsh_positional2 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings PASS; cargo fmt --check PASS.  {"id":"bd-3ohk","title":"Separation Logic proof annotations for TSM (section 5.8, Score 3.0)","description":"Add Separation Logic proof annotations to the TSM (Transparent Safety Membrane) codebase. Separation Logic extends Hoare Logic with spatial connectives (separating conjunction *, magic wand -*) that reason about heap-manipulating programs. For FrankenLibC, Separation Logic can prove: (1) Frame rule — TSM validation does not modify any memory outside its own bookkeeping region. Formally: {P * F} tsm_validate(ptr) {Q * F} — the frame F (all memory not accessed by TSM) is preserved. (2) Ownership transfer — when malloc returns a pointer, ownership transfers from the arena to the caller. When free is called, ownership transfers back. The generational arena's generation check is a runtime enforcement of the separation logic ownership discipline. (3) No-overlap — allocated regions do not overlap. Formally: {ptr1 |-> v1 * ptr2 |-> v2} implies ptr1 != ptr2. Implementation: (a) Annotate TSM functions with separation logic pre/post conditions as structured comments. (b) Use Prusti or Creusot (Rust verification tools) to mechanically check a subset. (c) Document which annotations are mechanically verified vs manually reviewed. Target: annotate all TSM entry points (validate_pointer, check_bounds, generation_check, quarantine_enter, repair_apply).

**Alien CS Reference:** Section 5.8 of the graveyard (Score 3.0). Separation Logic from Reynolds (2002) and O'Hearn (2001). Concurrent Separation Logic (CSL) from O'Hearn (2004). Iris (Birkedal et al.) provides a modern higher-order concurrent separation logic framework. For Rust: Prusti uses Viper backend, Creusot uses Why3.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- Define annotation macros: separation_logic_pre!/separation_logic_post! as structured doc comments parseable by external tools.
- For Prusti: use #[requires(...)] and #[ensures(...)] attributes on TSM functions.
- For Creusot: use #[creusot::requires(...)] with WhyML backend.
- Create separation_logic_audit.py CI script that extracts all annotations and verifies coverage.
- All unsafe blocks within annotated functions must have SAFETY comments referencing the separation logic justification.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
1. All 5 TSM entry points annotated with separation logic pre/post conditions.
2. At least 2 annotations mechanically verified by Prusti or Creusot (document which tool and any limitations).
3. Frame rule proven for tsm_validate: TSM does not modify caller-visible memory.
4. Ownership transfer annotations for malloc/free lifecycle: arena_owns(ptr) -> caller_owns(ptr) -> arena_owns(ptr).
5. separation_logic_audit.py runs in CI: reports annotated count, mechanically verified count, coverage percent.
6. Documentation committed to docs/separation_logic_annotations.md.
7. Unannotated TSM functions cause CI warning.

**Logging Requirements:**
- CI audit outputs structured JSON: {annotated: N, verified_mechanical: M, verified_manual: K, coverage_pct: P}.
- Any unannotated TSM function triggers CI warning with function name and file location.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

 {"id":"bd-3ot","title":"Runtime math production admission policy: value proofs, retirement, and annex routing","description":"Background:
- High-math controllers can be production-grade only when each retained controller shows measurable runtime value and explicit role in decision law.

Goal:
- Enforce production math admission policy with formal value proofs and conservative fail-safe behavior.

Deliverables:
1) Controller-by-controller production admission dossier:
   - decision-law hook,
   - invariants,
   - runtime cost,
   - measurable benefit vs ablated baseline.
2) Retirement/escalation policy for controllers failing value tests.
3) CI gate that blocks unsupported production math additions.
4) Research annex route for non-production controllers.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Every production controller has machine-checkable value evidence.
- No ambiguous “ornamental” modules in production path.
- Removal/retention decisions are reproducible and auditable.

Test and Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for controller manifest/policy validation.
- E2E tests for decision-law integrity under controller toggles.
- Structured logs for ablation/value runs with traceable metrics and decision outcomes.

Alien-artifact Skill Requirements:
- Preserve rigor and explainability simultaneously: evidence ledgers, bounded risk controls, and explicit assumptions for guarantees.

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Success Criteria

### Mandatory Verification Gate
1. Unit Test Evidence
- Add deterministic unit tests for nominal, boundary, and invalid-input paths in the touched modules.
- Include strict and hardened mode assertions when behavior differs by runtime mode.
- Include failure-mode tests covering repair/deny/default behavior with explicit expected outputs.

2. Integration + E2E Evidence
- Add or extend fixture-driven integration coverage in `tests/conformance` and/or `tests/integration` for this bead scope.
- Add a deterministic E2E script path (or harness command) with explicit setup/teardown and replayable seeds.
- Compare FrankenLibC outputs against host glibc fixtures (or documented replacement-level contract) for all touched symbols.

3. Logging + Traceability Evidence
- Emit structured logs (JSONL or deterministic text) that include bead ID, scenario ID, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, and outcome.
- Verify logs expose repair/deny counters and reason codes so regressions are diagnosable from artifacts alone.
- Record artifact paths in bead notes (test logs, fixture diffs, benchmark outputs, conformance reports).

4. Performance + Regression Evidence
- Capture before/after baseline for latency/throughput on touched hot paths.
- Enforce no-regression threshold or document bounded tradeoff with quantitative rationale.

5. CI Gate Evidence
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets`
- all bead-specific harness and E2E scripts pass with deterministic logs archived.","notes":"## Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- [ ] Unit tests added/updated for happy-path, edge-case, and invalid-input behavior.
- [ ] Property-style or fuzz-seed replay coverage added where state space is large/combinatorial.
- [ ] Integration fixtures added/updated in `tests/conformance` and validated against host glibc expectations.
- [ ] Deterministic E2E script/runner added or updated with explicit command lines and seed controls.
- [ ] Strict vs hardened behavior assertions are explicit and versioned in fixtures.
- [ ] Structured logs include: timestamp, bead ID, test scenario, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, action (allow/repair/deny), and result.
- [ ] Failure logs include reason codes + minimal reproducible input payload metadata.
- [ ] Benchmark/perf logs include baseline, candidate, delta %, and pass/fail gate decision.
- [ ] CI artifacts include conformance report, E2E transcript, and summarized pass/fail matrix.

2. Integration + E2E Evidence

- Add a deterministic E2E script path (or harness command) with explicit setup/teardown and replayable seeds.

3. Logging + Traceability Evidence

- Record artifact paths in bead notes (test logs, fixture diffs, benchmark outputs, conformance reports).

4. Performance + Regression Evidence
- Capture before/after baseline for latency/throughput on touched hot paths.
- Enforce no-regression threshold or document bounded tradeoff with quantitative rationale.

5. CI Gate Evidence

- `cargo test --all-targets`

 {"id":"bd-gcy","title":"bd-c1x subtask: Futex condvar core implementation (wait/signal/broadcast/timedwait)","description":"Background:
- Condvar implementation must coordinate with mutex ownership and futex wake ordering safely.

Goal:
- Implement futex-based condvar core with deterministic sequence and wake semantics.

Deliverables:
1) Internal condvar sequence/counter design.
2) wait path with unlock/relock choreography around futex_wait.
3) signal/broadcast wake policy.
4) Timed wait with absolute deadline handling.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Core condvar operations satisfy spec for success/error/timeout cases.
- No missed wakeups in deterministic stress tests.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests: signal-before-wait, wait-before-signal, broadcast-many, timeout races.
- Structured logs: trace_id, mode, condvar_state, mutex_state, wake_count, timeout_state, errno.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PurpleHawk","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:51.531693883Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:32.207008001Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:32.206927771Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","futex","implementation","pthread","testing"],"comments":[{"id":1088,"issue_id":"bd-gcy","author":"PurpleHawk","text":"Completed futex condvar core implementation (bd-gcy). Deliverables:

1) CondvarData repr(C) struct with seq counter, waiter count, associated mutex, and clock_id — overlays onto pthread_cond_t memory (20 bytes, fits in 48-byte struct).

2) condvar_wait(): atomic unlock-wait-relock choreography using sys_futex. Captures seq before unlock, parks with FUTEX_WAIT on seq word, relocks via CAS+futex loop on return. Validates mutex association invariant (EINVAL on mismatch).

3) condvar_timedwait(): uses FUTEX_WAIT_BITSET with absolute timeout. FUTEX_CLOCK_REALTIME flag selects clock domain matching condvar's configured clock. Returns ETIMEDOUT on deadline expiry after relocking mutex (per POSIX).

4) condvar_signal(): increments seq + futex_wake(1). condvar_broadcast(): increments seq + futex_wake(INT_MAX). Both are no-ops when no waiters (seq still incremented for consistency).

5) relock_mutex() helper: simple CAS loop with futex parking for relocking after condvar wait.

6) 64 total tests pass: 41 contract tests (bd-blg) + 23 core implementation tests including 4 threaded integration tests:
   - core_condvar_wait_signal_roundtrip (single waiter + signal)
   - core_condvar_broadcast_wakes_all (4 waiters + broadcast)
   - core_condvar_timedwait_expires (past deadline -> ETIMEDOUT)
   - core_condvar_mutex_association_mismatch (EINVAL on different mutex)

7) Exports from pthread module: CondvarData, condvar_init/destroy/wait/timedwait/signal/broadcast.

Verification: cargo test -p frankenlibc-core -- pthread::cond (64 passed, 0 failed). cargo check --all-targets clean.","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:26Z"}]}
